aquacroptools 0.1.0
  • Reference

Write AquaCrop Rainfall File

Source: R/write_plu.R
write_plu.Rd

Write an AquaCrop v7.0 (August 2022) rainfall (.PLU) file containing daily precipitation data for use in crop simulations.

Usage

write_plu(
  path = "weather/",
  stn = NULL,
  data = NULL,
  var_name = "rain",
  syear = NULL,
  eyear = NULL,
  eol = "windows",
  record_type = 1,
  first_day = 1,
  first_month = 1
)

Arguments

path

Directory path where the output .PLU file will be written. Default = "weather/"

stn

Station name or identifier for the weather station. Default = NULL (extracted from data if available)

data

Data frame containing daily rainfall data. Can have either:

  • Columns: station, year, month, day, rain (legacy format), or

  • Columns: year, month, day, rain (new format)

var_name

Name of the rainfall column in the data frame. Default = "rain"

syear

Start year of the data period (extracted from data if not provided). Default = NULL

eyear

End year of the data period (extracted from data if not provided). Default = NULL

eol

End-of-line character style for the output file. Options: "windows", "unix", "linux", or "macOS". Default = "windows"

record_type

Type of temporal aggregation. Options: 1 = daily, 2 = 10-daily, 3 = monthly. Default = 1

first_day

First day of record. For 10-daily: 1, 11, or 21; For monthly: 1. Default = 1

first_month

First month of record (1-12). Default = 1

Value

Invisibly returns the full path to the created .PLU file. Creates a formatted .PLU file in the specified directory with the format: <>.PLU containing daily rainfall data as required by AquaCrop v7.0.

See also

weather for example weather data, write_eto for writing ETo files, write_tnx for writing temperature files

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
  # Load example weather data
  data("weather")

  # Write rainfall file with default column name
  write_plu(
    path = "weather/",
    stn = "Wakanda_Station",
    data = weather
  )

  # Legacy format (backward compatibility)
  write_plu(data = weather_with_station, path = "climate/")
} # }
